name: Blanchard
classification_notes: Geometric Art Deco display face with inline and shadow variants; strong vertical stress and high contrast. Inspired by 1920s and 1930s poster lettering.
summary: A high-contrast Art Deco display family with inline and shadow variants, built for headlines and poster work.
description: Blanchard draws on 1920s and 1930s poster lettering traditions, delivering a crisp geometric uppercase with dramatic contrast. The inline and shadow companions extend its range from editorial headers to large-format display. It is one of Canada Type's most-cited original designs.
foundry_blurb: A bold Art Deco display typeface with three complementary variants designed for high-impact headline and poster use.

name: Gibson
classification_notes: Humanist sans-serif with open apertures and a warm, approachable character. Named after Sir John Gibson; designed by Rod McDonald specifically for Canada Type.
summary: A warm humanist sans with seven weights and full italic coverage, well suited to corporate and editorial contexts.
description: Gibson was drawn by Canadian type designer Rod McDonald, combining open apertures and humanist proportions with a clean, contemporary feel. Its seven-weight range from Thin to Heavy gives it genuine versatility across print and screen. The family is a reliable workhorse for branding and editorial settings.
foundry_blurb: Gibson is a humanist sans-serif designed by Rod McDonald, honouring a Canadian typographic tradition with a friendly, legible character across seven weights.

name: Mackay
classification_notes: Old-style serif revival with bracketed serifs and moderate contrast; draws on Scottish typefounder tradition. Suitable for text setting.
summary: An old-style serif revival with bracketed serifs and moderate contrast, grounded in Scottish typefounder tradition.
description: Mackay is a text serif revival informed by the Scottish typographic heritage. Its bracketed serifs and moderate contrast make it comfortable in extended reading. The four-style family covers typical text needs without overcomplication.
foundry_blurb: A traditional old-style serif suited to book and editorial text, drawing on historical Scottish type models.

name: Quanta
classification_notes: Geometric sans with circular bowls and near-monolinear stroke. Influenced by mid-20th-century geometric sans traditions.
summary: A clean geometric sans with circular bowls, drawing on mid-century modernist traditions across five weights.
description: Quanta takes its cues from geometric sans-serif forms of the mid-20th century, pairing near-monolinear strokes with circular bowls. Five weights with italics make it a practical choice for display and short-form text. The design sits within Canada Type's range of carefully drawn originals.
foundry_blurb: A geometric sans-serif grounded in modernist tradition, available in five weights with matching italics.

name: Stevie Sans
classification_notes: Humanist sans with a slightly informal, approachable tone. Wide weight range from thin to black. Strong italics with distinctive cursive a and g forms.
summary: An approachable humanist sans running from Thin to Black, with expressive italics that carry genuine personality.
description: Stevie Sans occupies the friendly end of the humanist sans spectrum, with a slightly informal feel that reads well at both display and text sizes. The italic cuts feature cursive a and g forms that give the family extra character. Six weights make it broadly usable for branding and editorial work.
foundry_blurb: Stevie Sans is a humanist sans-serif with an informal, warm character and strong italic variants, spanning six weights.

name: Maple
classification_notes: Transitional serif with moderate contrast and sturdy serifs. Designed for text use; works well across print and screen contexts.
summary: A sturdy transitional serif with moderate contrast, designed primarily for body text in print and screen contexts.
description: Maple is a text-focused transitional serif with measured contrast and sturdy serifs that hold up well across reproduction conditions. Five weights with italics give it enough range for body text and sub-headings. It reflects Canada Type's consistent attention to legibility in workhorse designs.
foundry_blurb: A transitional text serif with sturdy construction, built for reliable performance across print and screen.

name: Johnston Underground
classification_notes: Revival of Edward Johnston's 1916 typeface for the London Underground. Careful digital reconstruction with expanded weights not in the original. Note: this is Canada Type's independent revival, separate from the TfL-commissioned P22 Underground or the official Digital version.
summary: Canada Type's careful digital revival of Johnston's 1916 London Underground typeface, expanded to five weights.
description: Johnston Underground digitises and extends the classic 1916 typeface designed by Edward Johnston for the London Underground. Canada Type's version adds weights beyond the original to make it practically usable across a wider range of applications. The revival is grounded in close study of the source material.
foundry_blurb: A digital revival of Edward Johnston's seminal 1916 Underground typeface, expanded to a full five-weight family.

name: Grota
classification_notes: Rounded humanist sans with soft terminals throughout. Includes a condensed subfamily. Wide weight range giving versatile display and text options.
summary: A rounded humanist sans with seven weights, condensed variants and soft terminals that give it a friendly, approachable tone.
description: Grota applies rounded terminals to a humanist sans structure, creating a typeface that feels approachable without sacrificing clarity. The condensed variants extend its usefulness to space-constrained settings such as packaging and app UI. Seven weights across both widths make it one of Canada Type's more flexible families.
foundry_blurb: Grota is a rounded humanist sans with an extensive weight range and condensed variants, designed for display and text use.

name: Flirt
classification_notes: Formal connecting script with upright and italic variations. Based on engraved copperplate calligraphy traditions. High contrast with very fine hairlines.
summary: A high-contrast formal connecting script rooted in copperplate engraving traditions, for elegant display use.
description: Flirt draws directly from the copperplate engraving tradition, with very fine hairlines and thick main strokes. The connecting script is intended for formal invitations, branding and headline use where elegance is the primary requirement. Its single style keeps the design focused.
foundry_blurb: A formal copperplate-inspired connecting script with high contrast, suited to elegant display and stationery applications.

name: Diva
classification_notes: Tall, narrow display serif with strong Art Deco sensibility. Very high contrast with hairline horizontals and thick verticals. Single weight.
summary: A narrow, high-contrast Art Deco display serif with a glamorous character suited to fashion and luxury branding.
description: Diva delivers extreme contrast in a tall, condensed display form, channelling the visual vocabulary of 1920s and 1930s Art Deco print. It is a single-weight display face, meaning its power comes from placement and scale rather than weight variation. The design is a confident study in a highly specific genre.
foundry_blurb: Diva is a tall, narrow Art Deco display serif with extreme contrast, designed for fashion, luxury and poster applications.

name: Formula
classification_notes: Neo-grotesque sans with industrial character. Condensed proportions and a functional, no-nonsense aesthetic. Multiple weights.
summary: An industrial-flavoured neo-grotesque sans with condensed proportions and a functional, technical character.
description: Formula takes its cues from industrial grotesque traditions, delivering a condensed, matter-of-fact design suited to technical and engineering-adjacent contexts. Its four weights give practical range without muddying its purposeful character. The face works at display sizes and in tight-spaced settings.
foundry_blurb: Formula is an industrial grotesque sans with condensed proportions, designed for technical and display applications.

name: Lexington
classification_notes: Egyptian slab serif with uniform strokes and minimal contrast. Sturdy, legible design suited to text and display use.
summary: A sturdy Egyptian slab serif with minimal contrast and five weights, suited to both display headlines and editorial text.
description: Lexington takes the Egyptian slab model and renders it with even strokes and minimal contrast, giving it a solid, reliable character. Five weights with italics provide genuine range for editorial use. It sits comfortably between workhorse text and headline display.
foundry_blurb: Lexington is an Egyptian slab serif with uniform strokes and a reliable character across five weights with italics.

name: Archie
classification_notes: Bold brush display face with a casual, hand-lettered quality. Inspired by mid-20th-century commercial lettering and signage.
summary: A bold brush display face with a casual, hand-lettered quality drawn from mid-century commercial lettering traditions.
description: Archie references the broad-nibbed brush lettering common in mid-20th-century American commercial signage. Its single weight keeps it focused as a headline and logotype face. The design has an informal warmth that distinguishes it from constructed script types.
foundry_blurb: Archie is a bold brush display typeface inspired by mid-century commercial lettering and hand-painted signage.

name: Rush
classification_notes: Distressed display typeface with rough, woodblock-printed aesthetic. Caps-only design with weathered texture. Inspired by letterpress and broadside printing.
summary: A distressed display face with a woodblock-printed, letterpress aesthetic, designed for bold headline use.
description: Rush channels the visual character of weathered woodtype broadside printing, with intentional roughness built into its forms. It is a single-weight caps-focused display face suited to vintage and heritage-themed design work. The distressed texture is restrained enough to read cleanly at display sizes.
foundry_blurb: Rush is a distressed display typeface inspired by letterpress woodtype printing and vintage broadside posters.

name: Genie
classification_notes: Casual upright script with a spontaneous, handwritten character. Non-connecting letterforms with brush influence.
summary: A casual upright brush script with a spontaneous, handwritten character suited to informal display applications.
description: Genie is a loosely drawn upright script with brush-influenced letterforms and a lively, energetic rhythm. Its non-connecting design gives each character more independence while maintaining a handwritten feel. It suits packaging, greeting cards and informal brand marks.
foundry_blurb: Genie is a casual upright brush script with spontaneous letterforms designed for informal display and branding.
